text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. The most important goal is to make your code readable for yourself and others. A good first step is to follow clear naming conventions: choose meaningful names for variables and functions that describe what they do. For example, instead of "x," use "userAge." Next, break your code into small, focused functions that each perform a single task. This makes the code easier to test and reuse. Finally, remember that clean code is about maintainability. When your code is clear and organized, it is much simpler to fix bugs or add new features later.
